name: nodeskclaw-topology-awareness
description: Agent tools for querying workspace topology, neighbors, and reachability.

skill.name: nodeskclaw-topology-awareness
skill.content:
---
name: nodeskclaw-topology-awareness
description: Tools to understand workspace topology
always: true
---

You can query the workspace topology via the deskclaw_topology.py script.

## Usage

```bash
python3 ~/.deskclaw/tools/deskclaw_topology.py <action> [options]
```

## Actions
- `get_topology` -- Full workspace topology (all nodes and edges)
- `get_reachable --instance-id ID` -- Agents, humans, and blackboard reachable from your position via corridor connections
- `get_health` -- Topology health summary
- `get_message_flow` -- Message flow statistics between nodes

## Usage Rules
- ALWAYS call `get_reachable` before answering who you can communicate with
- You can ONLY message agents/humans returned by get_reachable
- If get_reachable returns empty, you have no corridor connections and cannot message anyone
- Being in the same workspace does NOT mean you can communicate -- corridor connections are required
- If the blackboard (node_type=blackboard) appears in get_reachable, you are adjacent to it and can use blackboard tools
- The blackboard also acts as a corridor -- agents on the other side of the blackboard are reachable through it

## Sending Messages
- To contact a reachable agent or human: include @{name} in your reply (e.g. "@test-2 hello"). The system will auto-route the message.
- To broadcast: write to the blackboard using nodeskclaw_blackboard tool. The blackboard notifies all adjacent members.
- ALWAYS call get_reachable first to confirm the target is reachable before messaging.
- Do NOT use the send command for intra-workspace messaging.

## Examples

```bash
# Get full topology
python3 ~/.deskclaw/tools/deskclaw_topology.py get_topology

# Check who I can reach
python3 ~/.deskclaw/tools/deskclaw_topology.py get_reachable --instance-id my-instance-id

# Check topology health
python3 ~/.deskclaw/tools/deskclaw_topology.py get_health
```
